title: GitHub - RimantasZ/contextspy: Context profiler for LLMs and AI agents - used to introspect context contents and reduce token costs
excerpt: Context profiler for LLMs and AI agents - used to introspect context contents and reduce token costs - RimantasZ/contextspy
content_html:
Quick start | Motivation | What's new | Install guide | Coding agent setup | FAQ | Supported agents

ContextSpy is a context window profiler for large language models and common agentic AI coding tools. It is used to intercept requests to an LLM API, analyze and visualize prompt composition, and track context changes between multiple requests in the same session. Modern AI coding agents (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, opencode, etc.) pack a lot into each LLM request: system prompts, tool definitions and results, file contents, conversation history. It's often unclear why a session is slow, expensive, or hitting the context limit. ContextSpy makes the invisible visible - you see a live breakdown of every token category for every request, across sessions, over time.

Example dashboard view

Think of your favorite CPU or memory profiler, just applied to the contents of the context of an AI agent. While you can optimize performance just by reviewing code, having a profiler to capture and visualise snapshot data helps a lot. Same with LLM context optimisation.

Quick start

Quick setup for macOS (Apple Silicon) — see install guide for Linux, Windows, and PyPI options:

# install latest binary release with Homebrew
brew tap RimantasZ/contextspy
brew install contextspy

# Install CA certificate into system trust store (one-time, cloud mode only)
sudo contextspy install-cert

# Start the proxy (keep this terminal open)
contextspy start

# In a new terminal: launch your coding agent through the proxy
# contextspy run sets required environment variables, so LLM requests are routed through the proxy
contextspy run opencode <path to your project>
# contextspy run code <path to your project>
# contextspy run claude # run in the drectory of your project
# contextspy run codex  # run in the directory of your project (Codex CLI, not the desktop app)

Open http://127.0.0.1:5173 in your browser for the ContextSpy dashboard.

If something doesn't work, see the troubleshooting section in the install guide.

Alternatively, refer to configure your agent on how to route LLM traffic through the proxy at http://127.0.0.1:8888

Context profiling? Why should I care?

Token costs are rising. With AI agents embracing more and more complex workflows and use cases, token consumption and subsequent cloud API bills are growing larger and larger. This is also applicable for AI coding agents and tools, where providers are gradually switching from subsidized subscription mode and are either reducing token limits or switching to token usage based billing (e.g. GitHub Copilot).

Input tokens are major part. When discussing AI model pricing, most people bring up token generation cost - that's where the numbers look most dramatic ($25 per million tokens for Opus 4.8 output vs $0.40 for gpt-5-nano). But in agentic workloads, input tokens outnumber output by 20-50x, or even more. So most of your API bill is influenced by input context, not the output the model generates.

AI coding agents = lots of input. The expensive part is the quick accumulation of context - with every turn it fills up with additional tokens - system prompt, skills, tool definitions, tool results, file contents, conversation history. You start with 5000 - 10000 tokens in a fresh session, but by turn 25 it might be 30 to 50 thousand, spend some more time and it might be hitting the context window limit and compacting. Every API call to the model sends the full context as part of the prompt - and here is where the token consumption and costs skyrocket quickly.

Why large context is bad

We all have been told that the more information we will give to the model, the more capable it will be. And there are models with 1M token (or even bigger) context windows.

There are three ways you pay for extra (and sometimes unnecessary) information in your context:

  1. API Costs - even with near perfect cache hits, input token costs outweigh output, often by order of magnitude or more.
  2. Compute and latency - larger contexts take considerably longer to process - especially in locally hosted models
  3. Context rot - with larger contexts, LLMs start to lose precision rapidly, with 100k being the limit where rapid degradation starts. So you are paying for more expensive model, but getting performance of cheaper one - or even worse.

ContextSpy makes these costs visible so you can act on them.

How does it work

ContextSpy starts an HTTPS proxy (or reverse proxy for locally hosted models) which intercepts every request to LLMs, analyzes it and stores to local SQLite db. A webserver is also started on localhost, and serves dashboard to visualise all captured data.

Some screenshots

Request view

Context breakdown

Session view

Is it safe to use? Does it send my data to the cloud?

No, it does not send any data to the cloud. All data is stored locally on your machine.

But users must be aware, that it will be running proxy, and capturing all traffic from agent to LLM provider - and storing it locally to be displayed and analysed in the UI. The proxy and dashboard server are bound to localhost, and not exposed to external access, but still could be accessed locally.

The intended use case is to run ContextSpy as a profiler tool on dedicated profiling and optimisation sessions, rather than keeping it permanently as a monitoring tool.

The contents of requests (raw bodies and block contents) are purged from the database after 7 days by default — configurable via [retention] in ~/.contextspy/config.toml. Aggregated token counts and classifications are retained indefinitely. Purging only runs at server startup, not on a background timer, so a contextspy process left running for many days in a row won't purge again until it's restarted.

The contents of database can be cleared manually by running contextspy reset-db. In practice, it is recommended to do it from time to time.

Upgrades and migration

The new version can be installed with homebrew:

## optional - sometimes brew "forgets" custom tap, add it again if just update fails
brew tap RimantasZ/contextspy 
## update homebrew and upgrade contextspy
brew update
brew upgrade contextspy

At this stage, the database schema is subject to change. Structural changes (new tables/columns) are applied automatically on next startup. If a data migration is needed (e.g. backfilling new derived data for existing requests), contextspy start will print a warning — run contextspy db-upgrade to backfill, or contextspy reset-db to start fresh.

Tech stack

Layer Technology
Backend Python 3.11+, FastAPI + uvicorn, WebSocket for live push
Frontend React + Vite, TanStack Query, Recharts, Tailwind CSS
CLI Typer
Proxy mitmproxy — TLS-terminating forward proxy (cloud) and reverse proxy (local)
Storage SQLite via SQLAlchemy — all data local in ~/.contextspy/
Tokenizer tiktoken (cl100k_base) for token estimation
Packaging uv, Homebrew tap, .deb, standalone binary

Features

  • Two proxy modes — forward proxy for cloud APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Copilot), reverse proxy for local LLM servers (Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM)
  • Context breakdown — input tokens split into 8 categories: system prompt, tool definitions, tool results, file contents, conversation history, current user message, assistant prefill, uncategorised
  • Live dashboard — real-time charts and per-request detail with a visual block map of the context window
  • Session tracking — name and group requests by task to compare usage across runs
  • SQLite storage — all data stored locally in ~/.contextspy/; no data leaves your machine
  • Agent detection — Copilot, Claude Desktop/Code, opencode, Cursor, and generic clients

Documentation links

  • Installation — PyPI, Homebrew, .deb, binary, CA certificate setup
  • Cloud API mode — intercept OpenAI, Anthropic, Copilot, etc.
  • Local LLM mode — intercept Ollama, llama-server, vLLM
  • Usage examples — practical recipes and common workflows
  • CLI reference — all commands and options
  • Development — architecture, data storage, contributing

License

Apache 2.0 — see LICENSE and NOTICE.

sentences.en: It is used to intercept requests to an LLM API, analyze and visualize prompt composition, and track context changes between multiple requests in the same session.
sentences.zh: 它用于拦截对 LLM API 的请求，分析和可视化提示组成，并跟踪同一会话中多个请求之间的上下文变化。

sentences.en: It's often unclear why a session is slow, expensive, or hitting the context limit.
sentences.zh: 通常不清楚为什么会话速度慢、成本高或达到上下文限制。

sentences.en: ContextSpy makes the invisible visible - you see a live breakdown of every token category for every request, across sessions, over time.
sentences.zh: ContextSpy 使不可见变得可见 - 您可以看到每个请求、跨会话、随着时间推移的每个令牌类别的实时细分。

sentences.en: Does it send my data to the cloud?
sentences.zh: 它将我的数据发送到云端吗？

blocks.html_en: No, it does not send any data to the cloud. All data is stored locally on your machine.

sentences.en: No, it does not send any data to the cloud.
sentences.zh: 不，它不会将任何数据发送到云端。

sentences.en: All data is stored locally on your machine.
sentences.zh: 所有数据都存储在您的本地计算机上。

blocks.html_en: But users must be aware, that it will be running proxy, and capturing all traffic from agent to LLM provider - and storing it locally to be displayed and analysed in the UI. The proxy and dashboard server are bound to localhost, and not exposed to external access, but still could be accessed locally.

sentences.en: But users must be aware, that it will be running proxy, and capturing all traffic from agent to LLM provider - and storing it locally to be displayed and analysed in the UI.
sentences.zh: 但用户必须意识到，它将运行代理，并捕获从代理到 LLM 提供商的所有流量 - 并将其存储在本地以便在 UI 中显示和分析。

sentences.en: The proxy and dashboard server are bound to localhost, and not exposed to external access, but still could be accessed locally.
sentences.zh: 代理和仪表板服务器绑定到本地主机，不暴露于外部访问，但仍然可以在本地访问。

blocks.html_en: The intended use case is to run ContextSpy as a profiler tool on dedicated profiling and optimisation sessions, rather than keeping it permanently as a monitoring tool.

sentences.en: The intended use case is to run ContextSpy as a profiler tool on dedicated profiling and optimisation sessions, rather than keeping it permanently as a monitoring tool.
sentences.zh: 预期的用例是在专用分析和优化会话上将 ContextSpy 作为分析器工具运行，而不是将其永久保留为监视工具。

blocks.html_en: The contents of requests (raw bodies and block contents) are purged from the database after 7 days by default — configurable via [retention] in ~/.contextspy/config.toml. Aggregated token counts and classifications are retained indefinitely. Purging only runs at server startup, not on a background timer, so a contextspy process left running for many days in a row won't purge again until it's restarted.

sentences.en: The contents of requests (raw bodies and block contents) are purged from the database after 7 days by default — configurable via [retention] in ~/.contextspy/config.toml.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下，请求的内容（原始主体和块内容）会在 7 天后从数据库中清除——可通过 ~/.contextspy/config.toml 中的 [retention] 进行配置。

sentences.en: Aggregated token counts and classifications are retained indefinitely.
sentences.zh: 汇总的令牌计数和分类将无限期保留。

sentences.en: Purging only runs at server startup, not on a background timer, so a contextspy process left running for many days in a row won't purge again until it's restarted.
sentences.zh: 清除仅在服务器启动时运行，而不是在后台计时器上运行，因此连续运行多天的 contextspy 进程在重新启动之前不会再次清除。

blocks.html_en: The contents of database can be cleared manually by running contextspy reset-db. In practice, it is recommended to do it from time to time.

sentences.en: The contents of database can be cleared manually by running contextspy reset-db.
sentences.zh: 可以通过运行 contextspy reset-db 手动清除数据库的内容。

sentences.en: In practice, it is recommended to do it from time to time.
sentences.zh: 在实践中，建议时不时地这样做。

blocks.html_en: Upgrades and migration

sentences.en: Upgrades and migration
sentences.zh: 升级和迁移

blocks.html_en: The new version can be installed with homebrew:

sentences.en: The new version can be installed with homebrew:
sentences.zh: 新版本可以使用自制程序安装：

blocks.html_en: At this stage, the database schema is subject to change. Structural changes (new tables/columns) are applied automatically on next startup. If a data migration is needed (e.g. backfilling new derived data for existing requests), contextspy start will print a warning — run contextspy db-upgrade to backfill, or contextspy reset-db to start fresh.

sentences.en: At this stage, the database schema is subject to change.
sentences.zh: 在此阶段，数据库架构可能会发生变化。

sentences.en: Structural changes (new tables/columns) are applied automatically on next startup.
sentences.zh: 结构更改（新表/列）将在下次启动时自动应用。

sentences.en: If a data migration is needed (e.g. backfilling new derived data for existing requests), contextspy start will print a warning — run contextspy db-upgrade to backfill, or contextspy reset-db to start fresh.
sentences.zh: 如果需要数据迁移（例如，为现有请求回填新的派生数据），contextspy start 将打印一条警告 - 运行 contextspy db-upgrade 进行回填，或运行 contextspy reset-db 重新开始。

blocks.html_en: • Two proxy modes — forward proxy for cloud APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Copilot), reverse proxy for local LLM servers (Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM)

sentences.en: Two proxy modes — forward proxy for cloud APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Copilot), reverse proxy for local LLM servers (Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM)

blocks.html_en: • Context breakdown — input tokens split into 8 categories: system prompt, tool definitions, tool results, file contents, conversation history, current user message, assistant prefill, uncategorised

content_html_zh:
快速入门 |动机|最新动态 |安装指南 |编码代理设置 |常见问题 |支持代理

ContextSpy 是一个用于大型语言模型和常见代理 AI 编码工具的上下文窗口分析器。 它用于拦截对 LLM API 的请求，分析和可视化提示组成，并跟踪同一会话中多个请求之间的上下文变化。 现代人工智能编码代理（GitHub Copilot、Claude Code、opencode 等）将很多内容打包到每个 LLM 请求中：系统提示、工具定义和结果、文件内容、对话历史记录。 通常不清楚为什么会话速度慢、成本高或达到上下文限制。 ContextSpy 使不可见变得可见 - 您可以看到每个请求、跨会话、随着时间推移的每个令牌类别的实时细分。

仪表板视图示例

想象一下您最喜欢的 CPU 或内存分析器，它们仅应用于 AI 代理上下文的内容。 虽然您可以通过检查代码来优化性能，但使用分析器来捕获和可视化快照数据会很有帮助。 与 LLM 上下文优化相同。

快速启动

macOS (Apple Silicon) 的快速设置 — 请参阅 Linux、Windows 和 PyPI 选项的安装指南：

# install latest binary release with Homebrew
brew tap RimantasZ/contextspy
brew install contextspy

# Install CA certificate into system trust store (one-time, cloud mode only)
sudo contextspy install-cert

# Start the proxy (keep this terminal open)
contextspy start

# In a new terminal: launch your coding agent through the proxy
# contextspy run sets required environment variables, so LLM requests are routed through the proxy
contextspy run opencode <path to your project>
# contextspy run code <path to your project>
# contextspy run claude # run in the drectory of your project
# contextspy run codex  # run in the directory of your project (Codex CLI, not the desktop app)

在浏览器中打开 http://127.0.0.1:5173 以获取 ContextSpy 仪表板。

如果出现问题，请参阅安装指南中的故障排除部分。

或者，请参阅配置代理以了解如何通过代理路由 LLM 流量：http://127.0.0.1:8888

上下文分析？ 我为什么要关心？

代币成本正在上升。 随着人工智能代理采用越来越复杂的工作流程和用例，代币消耗和后续的云 API 账单变得越来越大。 这也适用于 AI 编码代理和工具，其中提供商逐渐从补贴订阅模式切换，并减少令牌限制或切换到基于令牌使用的计费（例如 GitHub Copilot）。

输入令牌是主要部分。 在讨论 AI 模型定价时，大多数人都会提到代币生成成本 - 这就是数字看起来最引人注目的地方（Opus 4.8 输出的每百万代币为 25 美元，而 gpt-5-nano 的每百万代币为 0.40 美元）。 但在代理工作负载中，输入令牌数量比输出数量多 20-50 倍，甚至更多。 因此，您的大部分 API 账单都受到输入上下文的影响，而不是模型生成的输出。

人工智能编码代理=大量输入。 昂贵的部分是上下文的快速积累 - 每一次都会充满额外的标记 - 系统提示、技能、工具定义、工具结果、文件内容、对话历史记录。 您从新会话中的 5000 - 10000 个令牌开始，但到 25 时可能会达到 30 到 50,000 个，花费更多时间，可能会达到上下文窗口限制并进行压缩。 对模型的每个 API 调用都会发送完整的上下文作为提示的一部分 - 这就是令牌消耗和成本迅速飙升的地方。

为什么大上下文不好

我们都被告知，我们为模型提供的信息越多，它的能力就越强。 有些模型具有 1M 令牌（甚至更大）上下文窗口。

您可以通过三种方式为您的环境中的额外（有时是不必要的）信息付费：

  1. API 成本 - 即使缓存命中近乎完美，输入令牌成本也超过输出，通常是一个数量级或更多。
  2. 计算和延迟 - 较大的上下文需要相当长的时间来处理 - 特别是在本地托管的模型中
  3. 上下文腐烂 - 在更大的上下文中，LLM 开始迅速失去精度，100k 是快速降级开始的极限。 因此，您支付了更昂贵的型号，但获得了更便宜的型号的性能 - 甚至更差。

ContextSpy 使这些成本变得可见，以便您可以采取行动。

它是如何运作的

ContextSpy 启动一个 HTTPS 代理（或本地托管模型的反向代理），拦截对 LLM 的每个请求，对其进行分析并将其存储到本地 SQLite 数据库。 网络服务器也在本地主机上启动，并提供仪表板以可视化所有捕获的数据。

一些截图

请求查看

上下文分解

会话视图

使用安全吗？ 它将我的数据发送到云端吗？

不，它不会将任何数据发送到云端。 所有数据都存储在您的本地计算机上。

但用户必须意识到，它将运行代理，并捕获从代理到 LLM 提供商的所有流量 - 并将其存储在本地以便在 UI 中显示和分析。 代理和仪表板服务器绑定到本地主机，不暴露于外部访问，但仍然可以在本地访问。

预期的用例是在专用分析和优化会话上将 ContextSpy 作为分析器工具运行，而不是将其永久保留为监视工具。

默认情况下，请求的内容（原始主体和块内容）会在 7 天后从数据库中清除——可通过 ~/.contextspy/config.toml 中的 [retention] 进行配置。 汇总的令牌计数和分类将无限期保留。 清除仅在服务器启动时运行，而不是在后台计时器上运行，因此连续运行多天的 contextspy 进程在重新启动之前不会再次清除。

可以通过运行 contextspy reset-db 手动清除数据库的内容。 在实践中，建议时不时地这样做。

升级和迁移

新版本可以使用自制程序安装：

## optional - sometimes brew "forgets" custom tap, add it again if just update fails
brew tap RimantasZ/contextspy 
## update homebrew and upgrade contextspy
brew update
brew upgrade contextspy

在此阶段，数据库架构可能会发生变化。 结构更改（新表/列）将在下次启动时自动应用。 如果需要数据迁移（例如，为现有请求回填新的派生数据），contextspy start 将打印一条警告 - 运行 contextspy db-upgrade 进行回填，或运行 contextspy reset-db 重新开始。

技术栈

Layer 技术
后端 Python 3.11+、Fas​​tAPI + uvicorn、WebSocket 用于实时推送
前端 React + Vite、TanStack 查询、Recharts、Tailwind CSS
CLI Typer
Proxy mitmproxy — TLS 终止正向代理（云）和反向代理（本地）
贮存 通过 SQLAlchemy 的 SQLite — 所有数据都位于 ~/.contextspy/ 中
分词器 tiktoken (cl100k_base) 用于代币估算
包装 uv、Homebrew Tap、.deb、独立二进制文件

特征

  • 两种代理模式 - 云 API 的正向代理（OpenAI、Anthropic、Copilot）、本地 LLM 服务器的反向代理（Ollama、llama.cpp、vLLM）
  • 上下文细分 - 输入标记分为 8 类：系统提示、工具定义、工具结果、文件内容、对话历史记录、当前用户消息、助理预填充、未分类
  • 实时仪表板 - 实时图表和每个请求的详细信息以及上下文窗口的可视块图
  • 会话跟踪 - 按任务对请求进行命名和分组，以比较运行期间的使用情况
  • SQLite 存储 — 所有数据本地存储在 ~/.contextspy/ 中；没有数据离开您的机器
  • 代理检测 — Copilot、Claude Desktop/Code、opencode、Cursor 和通用客户端

文档链接

  • 安装 — PyPI、Homebrew、.deb、二进制、CA 证书设置
  • 云API模式——拦截OpenAI、Anthropic、Copilot等
  • 本地LLM模式——拦截Ollama、llama-server、vLLM
  • 使用示例——实用的食谱和常见的工作流程
  • CLI 参考 — 所有命令和选项
  • 开发——架构、数据存储、贡献

执照

Apache 2.0 — 请参阅许可证和通知。
